summary: Strong Estimated Prophet & Not Fade Away amid uneven Oakland set
blurb: The show delivers a standout Estimated Prophet and an inspired Not Fade Away jam, with Let The Good Times Roll opening the first set. Jerry’s playing swings between tired vocals and fire in the later jams, while Vince’s piano adds classy texture. Overall the concert feels uneven but offers several memorable moments.
review: February 26, 1995 at the Oakland‑Alameda County Coliseum was a mixed bag that still manages to produce a few gems worth chasing. The night opens with a rare Let The Good Times Roll, setting an optimistic tone before the set slides into a shaky West L.A. Fadeaway and a lackluster Lazy River Road. By the second set the band finds its footing: David Murray’s guest sax breathes new life into the rhythm section, propelling Estimated Prophet into an incendiary NFA climax, and the extended Not Fade Away jam showcases Garcia’s guitar sparking back to form. A brief acoustic interlude from Bob Weir adds a nice contrast, while Vince Welnick’s straight‑piano texture holds everything together. Though many songs suffer from flat tempos, weak vocals and mix issues – especially in monitor recordings – the highlights are strong enough that fans will want to hear this show, preferably from the clean soundboard source.

rating_calculation.sentiment_rationale: Across the five recordings, sentiment is mixed but leans positive: one recording is clearly positive (highlighting Estimated Prophet and NFA), one negative, and three mixed. Positive remarks about standout jams appear in multiple sources, outweighing the negatives enough for a modest boost.

best_recording.reason: Good‑quality soundboard mix with clear balance, tight rhythm section and the praised Estimated Prophet/NFA climax; captures the band at its clearest.

key_highlights: Estimated Prophet jam building into an explosive NFA climax with David Murray’s sax.
Not Fade Away extended jam showcases Garcia’s late‑show guitar spark.
Let The Good Times Roll opens set uniquely, and Vince’s piano adds classy texture.

band_performance.Jerry: Garcia’s performance was uneven – his voice sounded tired and out‑of‑key on several numbers, yet his guitar sparked during the later jams like Not Fade Away and Estimated Prophet.
band_performance.Bob: Weir added an impromptu acoustic‑guitar trio that stood out, though his resonator was buried in the monitor mixes.
band_performance.Vince: Vince’s straight piano tone gave the band a classy, full texture; his solo earned praise while his vocal contribution on Good Times Roll drew mixed reactions.
band_performance.Mickey: The drums sounded tighter than usual, delivering a powerful backbone throughout the second set.
band_performance.Billy: Kreutzmann’s drumming locked in with Mickey, providing a solid rhythmic foundation that helped lift the jam sections.
